dictionary_sections.definition: EID Source Alignment Review Batch is a source-backed Opera Omnia registry packet centered on the canonical relation "reveals them by changing ε by \~10⁻⁷ over 27.32 days.". It identifies the mathematical spine of the cited chapter and gives the entry a concrete equation, symbol key, and derivation chain for the four-page production layout.

dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus:
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=6pt,topsep=4pt]
\item Canonical carrier: the selected formula that anchors the entry.
\item Source context: the chapter prose that explains why the formula belongs in the registry.
\item TRAWIN closure: the reading of the formula through time, rotation, action, wave, integration, and normalization.
\end{enumerate}
